threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still stand at the bus stop, not knowing how to hail a cab with a whip of your hand. Everyone else just raises an eyebrow.
Caught a guy on the 133 last night who was rapping his life story to the driver. Didn't skip a beat as we rolled past Electric Avenue.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw an entire family of ducks waddling down the Karl-Marx-Strasse, stopping traffic like they owned the place. Only in Neukölln!

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you see people lounging on urban playgrounds, sipping coffee while kids swing nearby, making even the swings feel like a chill hangout spot.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they automatically brace themselves as they approach BART after 5 PM, like it's a rollercoaster ride.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a man in a tuxedo casually hopping on the 19 bus at 2 AM, clutching a bouquet. Only in the Tenderloin would you see style mixed with late-night chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the street performances are spontaneous, but most have permits and schedules. The real magic is in the quieter corners where locals gather.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still trying to navigate the metro map, staring at it like it’s a puzzle, while locals rush by without a glance.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the ‘Overground’ instead of just ‘the train’ like the rest of us. Those early days are cute.
Caught a night bus home last week and the driver was blasting reggae. Entire bus singing along, made the 30-minute wait for my stop feel like a party.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When the smell of fresh döner wafts through your window at 2 AM, and you realize it’s just another night in the Kiez.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a Döner shop that serves a limited edition hummus-flavored kebab. It's like they keep inventing new food just to keep us guessing.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local when they know to avoid BART during rush hour. Only tourists are still shocked by the delays. It's just part of the grind.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Watched a guy on a Muni bus serenade everyone with a ukulele, only to get off at the next stop and disappear into the fog. Classic SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia's plaza is just for selfies, but locals use it for late-night chess games and impromptu gigs. It's a living room, not a backdrop.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their hesitation at every traffic light—standing still like they're waiting for a pedestrian signal that doesn’t exist.
